title: crepis sulphurea
intro: Crepis sulphurea, also known as Saffron Ragwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and leaves are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Crepis sulphurea is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion for its health benefits, and can also be used as a diuretic to treat urinary problems.
